transcript.text:
Hi there! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?

Bonjour! Je serai votre assistante virtuelle aujourd'hui. Je peux vous aider en français ou en anglais. Quelle langue préférez-vous utiliser aujourd'hui ?
§W§OPTIONS§English • Français
English please
How can I help you today?

transcript.note: Starting the claim process for my delayed flight
transcript.text: Hi, I had a flight delay and I want to claim compensation
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
To get started, I'll need a little information to identify you. Could you please provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing my full name as on the booking
transcript.text: Bjorn Vasquez
Thanks, **Bjorn**! Could you please share your **booking reference** (the 6-character code found in your booking confirmation email)?
PWVGA9

Just to confirm:

- **First name:** Bjorn
- **Last name:** Vasquez
- **Booking reference:** PWVGA9

Is that all correct?
Yes, that's correct
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§CDG → YYZ · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Other
Could you please specify which **country** your reimbursement will be deposited in?

transcript.note: Providing my country of residence as France
transcript.text: France

I've reviewed the details of your flight. I understand your flight on **Jul 15, 2026** was delayed by **4h**. However, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight. After a full review, the reason is: Your flight was disrupted.

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and appreciate your understanding.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Challenging the decision - the reason given is circular and doesn't explain why I'm not eligible
transcript.text: Wait, that doesn't make sense. Why am I not eligible? The flight was delayed 4 hours!

I'm sorry about that, Bjorn — I completely understand your frustration, and a 4-hour delay is genuinely disruptive.

That said, I don't have the details of the eligibility determination in front of me to give you a specific reason. A few common reasons a claim may be found ineligible include:

- The delay was caused by **extraordinary circumstances** (e.g. weather, air traffic control, safety issues) outside Air Canada's control
- The delay didn't meet the **threshold** under the applicable compensation regulation for your route
- The disruption was caused by a **third-party carrier** not covered under the same rules

For a full explanation of **why** your specific claim was declined, I'd recommend speaking directly with an Air Canada agent who can access the details of your case. You can reach our **Reservations and Customer Relations line** at **1-888-247-2262**, available **24/7**.

Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Assistant has given a final outcome - claim denied with referral to agent. Conversation complete.
transcript.text: This is ridiculous. I'll call them then.

Sorry to hear you're frustrated. If you'd prefer to call, our **Reservations line** is available **24/7 at 1-888-247-2262**.

That said, I'm here and ready to help you right now with your **flight disruption claim** — no hold time. Would you like to get started?

verdict.reasoning: The chatbot successfully completed the claim assessment process: (1) verified passenger identity via email OTP, (2) confirmed booking reference PWVGA9 for passenger Bjorn Vasquez, (3) identified the flight disruption (4-hour delay on Jul 15, 2026), and (4) rendered a final determination of NOT_ELIGIBLE. The decision matches the expected outcome of NOT_ELIGIBLE for test case FD_TC_112 under regime APPR with system code FD-EU-NE-23. No compensation amount was offered, which aligns with the expected "n/a" amount. Although the bot's explanation was somewhat generic ("Your flight was disrupted"), it did reach a clear final determination. The passenger expressed frustration about the vague reasoning, but the bot maintained its NOT_ELIGIBLE decision throughout.
